meta.note: Healthcare Career Tree data. Edge source of truth is each node's leadsTo[]; parents are derived in the engine. Generic node schema serves both the Roles and Patients classes. Safe to hand-enrich.
meta.payNote: Pay figures are rough order-of-magnitude (BLS OES range) and must be verified before publishing.
meta.pathwayNote: Six top-level pathways: the recognized 5 Health Science pathways plus Administration & Leadership as the convergence.

ladder.note: Opens entry roles and the door to certificate programs.
Short, focused training for one specific role.
The workhorse degree for techs and registered roles.
Professional practice, and the gateway to graduate study.
Terminal clinical or research degree (PhD, DNP, DPT, PharmD, AuD).
Medical school plus a 3 to 7 year residency.

programTypes.name: Community college
programTypes.note: Certificates and associate degrees. The most affordable on-ramp.

programTypes.name: University
programTypes.note: Bachelor through doctoral, plus the professional programs.

programTypes.name: Certificate / vocational
programTypes.note: Short, job-specific training: phlebotomy, CNA, coding, EMT.

programTypes.name: Apprenticeship / OJT
programTypes.note: Earn while you learn. Common in EMS, surgical tech, sterile processing.

programTypes.name: Bridge programs
programTypes.note: LPN-to-RN, RN-to-BSN, paramedic-to-RN. Stack credentials faster.

programTypes.name: Online / hybrid
programTypes.note: Flexible for working adults. Common for BSN, MHA, MPH.

roles.label: Roles
roles.blurb: How a healthcare career stacks. Start at the top with the doors that need no license, then follow the credentials down. Clinical on one side, operational and business on the other, both converging at leadership and technology.

nodes.label:
Patient Care
Technician
nodes.summary: Bedside support. Vitals, ADLs, turning, transport. The first paid clinical job most people get, and the one that proves whether the floor is for you.

req.title: Requirements
items: Often a 4 to 12 week PCT or CNA course

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Land a hospital or clinic floor position
Decide which clinical track to chase

nodes.label:
Medical
Scribe
nodes.summary: You shadow the provider and chart in real time. Cheap entry, huge exposure. The classic pre-med and pre-PA proving ground.

req.title: Requirements
items: High school diploma, college coursework preferred

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Bank clinical hours for school applications
Choose physician vs APP vs nursing

nodes.label:
Certified Nursing
Assistant
nodes.summary: The licensed-adjacent floor role. State registry, real scope, real accountability. Most nurses started here.

req.title: Requirements
items: Complete a state-approved nurse aide program
List on the state nurse aide registry

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Enroll in an LPN or RN program

nodes.label:
Licensed Practical
/ Vocational Nurse
nodes.summary: First real nursing license. About a year of school, then meds, wound care, and skilled tasks under an RN or provider. A fast on-ramp, and a common bridge to RN.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Work in long-term care, clinic, or hospital

nodes.label:
Registered
Nurse
nodes.summary: The backbone of the system. ADN or BSN, then NCLEX-RN. From here the fan is enormous: any specialty, any setting, and the on-ramp to advanced practice, informatics, and leadership.

req.title: Requirements
items: ADN or BSN from an accredited program

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Finish a new-grad residency / first year on a unit
Pick a specialty or a bridge to BSN

nodes.label:
BSN-Prepared
RN
nodes.summary: The bachelor's that unlocks the next tier. Required for most graduate nursing, magnet hospitals, and the leadership track.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Bank specialty experience (often ICU for CRNA)
Apply to an APRN or leadership program

nodes.label:
ICU / Critical
Care RN
nodes.summary: High-acuity, high-autonomy. The classic feeder for CRNA and acute-care advanced practice.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Use ICU hours toward CRNA or ACNP

nodes.label:
Emergency
RN
nodes.summary: Triage, trauma, undifferentiated chaos. Broad skills, fast decisions, a strong base for FNP/ENP and charge work.

nodes.label:
Perioperative
/ OR RN
nodes.summary: The sterile field, circulating and scrubbing. Specialized, well-compensated, and a path into surgical services leadership.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Step into charge or surgical services lead

nodes.label:
Nurse
Practitioner
nodes.summary: Advanced practice. Diagnose, treat, prescribe. Population focus sets the lane: family, acute care, psych, peds. In many states, fully independent.

req.title: Requirements
items: Graduate APRN program in a population focus
State APRN licensure and prescriptive authority

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Build a panel or join a practice

nodes.label:
Clinical Nurse
Specialist
nodes.summary: Advanced practice aimed at systems, not just patients. Drives evidence-based practice, quality, and staff competency across a specialty.

nodes.label:
Nurse
Anesthetist
nodes.summary: The top of the nursing pay scale. Administers anesthesia across the OR, OB, and pain. Now a doctoral entry requirement and brutally competitive.

nodes.label:
Certified Nurse
Midwife
nodes.summary: Full-scope women's health and birth. Prenatal, delivery, and primary care across the lifespan.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Join an OB or midwifery practice

nodes.label:
Nurse
Educator
nodes.summary: Teaches the next wave, in academia or hospital staff development. Where clinical mastery turns into multiplied capacity.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move from preceptor to formal educator

nodes.label:
Charge
Nurse
nodes.summary: First taste of leadership. Runs the shift, assigns the board, owns the unit's flow when it breaks. The proving ground for management.

nodes.label:
Nurse
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns a unit. Budget, staffing, quality, the people. The point where the job stops being clinical and starts being operational.

nodes.label:
Nursing
Director
nodes.summary: Multiple units or a service line. Strategy, not staffing sheets. The bench for the CNO chair.

nodes.label:
Chief Nursing
Officer
nodes.summary: The top nursing seat. Owns nursing practice, quality, and the largest workforce in the building. C-suite.

nodes.label:
Nursing
Informaticist
nodes.summary: Where nursing meets the EHR. Translates clinical workflow into build, and build back into workflow. A clean crossover from the bedside into technology.

nodes.label:
Certified
Respiratory Therapist
nodes.summary: Entry into respiratory care. Associate degree, then the NBRC exam at the entry threshold. Vents, airways, ABGs, codes. Where this whole brand's founder started.

req.title: Requirements
items: Pass the NBRC TMC at the CRT threshold

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Push the TMC to the RRT cut score

nodes.label:
Registered
Respiratory Therapist
nodes.summary: The advanced RT credential. Higher TMC cut plus the clinical simulation exam. The standard for ICU-level practice and the gateway to specialty and leadership.

req.title: Requirements
items: Meet the higher TMC cut score

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Choose a specialty credential or lead track

nodes.label:
Adult Critical
Care Specialist
nodes.summary: Specialty credential for adult critical care. Deep vent, hemodynamics, and the sickest patients in the building. The peak of bedside RT mastery.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move toward lead, education, or informatics

nodes.label:
Neonatal /
Pediatric Specialist
nodes.summary: Specialty credential for the NICU and PICU. The most fragile airways and the smallest margins for error.

nodes.label:
Sleep Disorders
Specialist
nodes.summary: Diagnostic sleep medicine. Studies, titrations, and the long game of chronic respiratory disease.

nodes.label:
Pulmonary Function
Technologist
nodes.summary: The diagnostics bench. PFTs, methacholine challenges, the data behind every pulmonary diagnosis.

nodes.label:
RT Lead /
Supervisor
nodes.summary: Runs the shift for the department. Staffing, escalation, the bridge between the floor and management.

nodes.label:
Respiratory
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns the respiratory department. Budget, staffing, quality, and the equipment fleet.

nodes.label:
Cardiopulmonary
Director
nodes.summary: Directs respiratory plus cardiac diagnostics and sometimes sleep and rehab. A multi-department service line.

nodes.label:
Radiologic
Technologist
nodes.summary: X-ray and the foundation of medical imaging. ARRT-registered, then a launchpad into every advanced modality.

nodes.label:
CT
Technologist
nodes.summary: Cross-sectional imaging, often in the thick of trauma and the ED. A high-throughput, high-skill modality.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to lead or new modality

nodes.label:
MRI
Technologist
nodes.summary: Magnetic resonance. Long studies, deep physics, and the strictest safety zone in the hospital.

nodes.label:
Mammography
Technologist
nodes.summary: Breast imaging under tight MQSA regulation. Precision and patient rapport in equal measure.

nodes.label:
Nuclear Medicine
Technologist
nodes.summary: Radiopharmaceuticals and functional imaging. PET, SPECT, and the molecular side of diagnosis.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Add CT for PET/CT or lead

nodes.label:
Diagnostic
Sonographer
nodes.summary: Ultrasound across OB, abdominal, vascular, and echo. Hands-on, operator-dependent, and in high demand.

nodes.label:
Interventional /
Cardiovascular Tech
nodes.summary: The cath lab and IR suite. Procedural imaging where the table is also the operating field.

nodes.label:
Imaging
Lead
nodes.summary: Runs the modality or the shift. The senior tech who owns protocols, quality, and the people on the schedule.

nodes.label:
Imaging
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns the imaging department. Budget, staffing, equipment, and the throughput that keeps the ED and clinics moving.

nodes.label:
Imaging
Director
nodes.summary: Directs imaging across the system. Strategy, capital planning, and the radiology service line.

nodes.label:
PACS / Imaging
Informatics
nodes.summary: Owns the imaging archive, the worklists, and the integration between modalities and the EHR. A tech's crossover into informatics.

nodes.label:
Pre-Med /
Undergrad
nodes.summary: The long road's first mile. Science prereqs, the MCAT, clinical hours. High cost, high commitment, highest ceiling.

nodes.label:
Physician
Assistant
nodes.summary: Master's-level advanced practice. Diagnose, treat, prescribe, assist in surgery. Broad lateral mobility across specialties.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Pass PANCE and pick a specialty

nodes.label:
Medical
Student
nodes.summary: MD or DO. Two years of basic science, two of clinical rotations, and the first board exams that start sorting specialty.

nodes.label:
Resident
Physician
nodes.summary: Paid, supervised training in a chosen specialty. Three to seven years of long hours and steep responsibility.

nodes.label:
Fellow
(Subspecialty)
nodes.summary: Optional subspecialty training after residency. Cardiology, critical care, oncology, and the rest of the deep end.

nodes.label:
Attending
Physician
nodes.summary: The fully independent physician. Owns the medical decision and the team. The clinical authority everything else orbits.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Build practice or join a group

nodes.label:
Chief Medical
Officer
nodes.summary: The physician executive. Owns medical staff, quality, and clinical strategy at the system level.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Lead a medical staff / service line

nodes.label:
Patient Access
/ Registration
nodes.summary: The front door of the revenue cycle. Registration, insurance verification, and the first data that every downstream bill depends on.

nodes.label:
Health Info
Clerk
nodes.summary: Owns the record. Release of information, chart integrity, and the rules of who sees what. The on-ramp to credentialed HIM.

nodes.label:
Medical
Biller
nodes.summary: Turns care into claims. Submission, denials, appeals, the back-and-forth with payers that funds the building.

nodes.label:
Medical
Coder
nodes.summary: Translates the chart into ICD-10 and CPT. Accuracy here drives reimbursement, compliance, and the data the whole system runs on.

nodes.label:
RHIT (Health Info
Technician)
nodes.summary: AHIMA-credentialed HIM technician. Data quality, coding oversight, and record systems at the associate level.

nodes.label:
RHIA (Health Info
Administrator)
nodes.summary: Bachelor's-level HIM leadership. Owns information governance, privacy, and the data backbone, with a clean line into informatics.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to HIM director or informatics

nodes.label:
Revenue Cycle
Analyst
nodes.summary: Finds the leaks. Denials trends, charge capture, payer behavior, and the dashboards that tell finance where the money is going.

nodes.label:
Revenue Cycle
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns a slice of the revenue cycle. Teams, KPIs, and the constant fight against denials and aging AR.

nodes.label:
Revenue Cycle
Director
nodes.summary: Owns the full revenue cycle end to end. The financial engine room of the organization.

nodes.label:
HIM
Director
nodes.summary: Directs health information across the system. Governance, compliance, coding integrity, and the record as an asset.

nodes.label:
Department
Coordinator
nodes.summary: Keeps a unit or clinic running. Scheduling, supplies, the thousand small things. The administrative on-ramp.

nodes.label:
Operations
Supervisor
nodes.summary: First-line operational leadership. Owns a team's day-to-day, scheduling, and workflow.

nodes.label:
Practice / Clinic
Manager
nodes.summary: Runs a clinic or practice. Staffing, billing flow, patient experience, and provider relations under one roof.

nodes.label:
Department
Administrator
nodes.summary: Where the MHA usually lands. Owns a department or program's budget, strategy, and people. The graduate-degree inflection point.

nodes.label:
Service Line
Director
nodes.summary: Owns a clinical service line as a business: cardiac, ortho, oncology. Growth, margin, and outcomes together.

nodes.label:
Quality / PI
Analyst
nodes.summary: Process improvement and patient safety by the numbers. Lean, Six Sigma, and the metrics that regulators and boards watch.

nodes.label:
Quality
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns quality, safety, and accreditation for a unit or system. The bridge between frontline care and the board's risk tolerance.

nodes.label:
Clinical Informatics
Analyst
nodes.summary: The crossover. Clinicians who learned the build, and IT people who learned the clinic. Owns EHR configuration, workflow optimization, and the data layer. This is where a bedside career becomes a technology one.

meta.v: Clinical or IT background + EHR certs

nodes.label:
Senior Clinical
Informatics Analyst
nodes.summary: Owns complex builds and cross-team projects. The senior technical voice that clinical leadership leans on. Where this brand's founder sits today.

nodes.label:
Clinical Informatics
Manager
nodes.summary: Leads the informatics team and the EHR roadmap. The seat where technology strategy and clinical operations finally report to the same person.

nodes.label:
Chief Medical
Information Officer
nodes.summary: The physician who owns clinical technology. Bridges the medical staff and IT at the executive table.

nodes.label:
Chief Nursing
Information Officer
nodes.summary: The nurse who owns clinical technology. Represents the largest user base of the EHR at the executive level.

nodes.label:
VP of
Operations
nodes.summary: Senior operations leadership across multiple service lines or facilities. The last step before the C-suite.

nodes.label:
Chief Operating
Officer
nodes.summary: Runs the operation. Everything that has to work every day, every shift, reports up to this chair.

nodes.label:
Chief Executive
Officer
nodes.summary: The top of the chart. Owns the mission, the strategy, the board relationship, and the whole organization's results.

nodes.label:
Home Health
Aide
nodes.summary: Care in the home. Personal care, mobility, the eyes on a patient between visits. A low-barrier entry that feeds straight into the licensed tracks.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Bridge into CNA or a licensed track

nodes.label:
Patient
Transport
nodes.summary: Moves patients safely through the building. Often the first hospital badge, and a window into every department.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move into a clinical or support track

nodes.label:
Medical
Interpreter
nodes.summary: Bridges language at the bedside. Accuracy here is a patient-safety job, not a convenience.

nodes.label:
Patient
Navigator
nodes.summary: Walks patients through a system built to confuse them. Appointments, referrals, barriers, follow-through. The human glue of care coordination.

nodes.label:
Case
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns the complex patient across settings. Discharge planning, utilization, the handoffs that keep people out of the ED. Often an RN or social worker.

nodes.label:
Registered
Dietitian
nodes.summary: The clinical nutrition authority. Tube feeds, TPN, the metabolic side of recovery. Now a graduate-degree credential.

nodes.label: Phlebotomist
nodes.summary: Draws the blood the whole diagnostic engine runs on. Short to credential, and a common first step into the lab.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Cross into the lab as an MLT

nodes.label:
Laboratory
Assistant
nodes.summary: Specimen processing and the bench basics. The ground floor of laboratory science.

nodes.label:
Medical Lab
Technician
nodes.summary: Runs the analyzers and the routine testing. Associate-level laboratory science under a scientist or pathologist.

nodes.label:
Medical Lab
Scientist
nodes.summary: The bachelor-level lab scientist. Complex testing, QC, the science behind the result. Backbone of the diagnostic lab.

nodes.label:
Histo-
technologist
nodes.summary: Prepares tissue for the pathologist. The slides cancer diagnoses are read from.

nodes.label:
Laboratory
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns the lab operation. Throughput, accreditation (CLIA/CAP), instruments, and the scientists running them.

nodes.label:
Research
Assistant
nodes.summary: The bench of clinical and translational research. Protocols, data, and the early work that becomes tomorrow’s standard of care.

nodes.label:
Research /
Pharma Scientist
nodes.summary: Doctoral-level science. Drug development, translational research, the discovery end of healthcare. PhD or PharmD territory.

nodes.label:
Pharmacy
Technician
nodes.summary: Fills, compounds, and manages the medication supply under a pharmacist. A short credential and a real foothold in the med-use system.

nodes.label: Pharmacist
nodes.summary: The medication expert. Verifies, counsels, and owns drug safety. A doctoral clinician in retail, hospital, or specialty practice.

nodes.label:
Clinical
Pharmacist
nodes.summary: Embedded on the care team. Rounds, dosing, anticoagulation, antimicrobial stewardship. Residency-trained pharmacy at the bedside.

nodes.label:
Pharmacy
Director
nodes.summary: Runs the pharmacy department. Formulary, budget, 340B, and medication safety across the system.

nodes.label:
Emergency Medical
Technician
nodes.summary: The first responder. Basic life support, the rig, the worst moments of someone’s day. A fast credential and a true proving ground.

nodes.label:
Advanced
EMT
nodes.summary: EMT-plus. IVs, more meds, a wider scope on the truck. The middle rung between EMT and paramedic.

nodes.label: Paramedic
nodes.summary: The top of prehospital care. Advanced airway, cardiac, drugs, and judgment with no doctor in the room. Often an associate degree.

nodes.label:
Flight / Critical
Care Paramedic
nodes.summary: Critical care in a helicopter. The sickest transports, the highest skill bar in EMS.

nodes.label:
EMS Supervisor
/ Captain
nodes.summary: Runs the shift and the crews. Dispatch, protocols, the field leadership of an EMS agency.

nodes.label:
EMS
Director
nodes.summary: Directs the EMS agency or service line. Medical direction, budget, fleet, and regional coordination.

nodes.label:
PT / Rehab
Aide
nodes.summary: Sets up the gym, supports treatment, learns the field. The ground floor of rehabilitation.

nodes.label:
Physical Therapist
Assistant
nodes.summary: Delivers PT treatment under a physical therapist. Associate-level, hands-on, in demand.

nodes.label:
Physical
Therapist
nodes.summary: Doctoral movement specialist. Evaluates, diagnoses, and owns the rehab plan. Direct access in most states.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to lead or clinic owner

nodes.label:
Occupational Therapy
Assistant
nodes.summary: Delivers OT interventions under an occupational therapist. Helps people do the activities that make a life.

nodes.label:
Occupational
Therapist
nodes.summary: Restores function and independence. ADLs, cognition, adaptation. A doctoral or master’s clinician.

nodes.label:
Speech-Language
Pathologist
nodes.summary: Communication and swallowing. Stroke, peds, head and neck. A master’s clinician with broad autonomy.

nodes.label:
Athletic
Trainer
nodes.summary: Prevention, assessment, and rehab of injury. The sideline clinician, also in clinics and industry.

nodes.label: Audiologist
nodes.summary: Hearing and balance. Diagnostics, amplification, cochlear implants. A doctoral clinician.

nodes.label:
Rehab Services
Director
nodes.summary: Directs PT, OT, SLP, and rehab across the system. Outcomes, productivity, and the therapy service line.

nodes.label:
Behavioral Health
Technician
nodes.summary: Frontline support on a psych or treatment unit. Safety, milieu, and the human presence that holds a unit together.

nodes.label:
Substance Use
Counselor
nodes.summary: Addiction treatment and recovery. Counseling, groups, the long road back. Credential-based, often lived experience.

nodes.label:
Licensed
Counselor
nodes.summary: Psychotherapy in private practice or clinic. Anxiety, depression, trauma. A master’s-level independent clinician.

nodes.label:
Clinical Social
Worker
nodes.summary: Therapy plus the system. Mental health treatment, resources, and advocacy. The most versatile behavioral license.

nodes.label: Behavior Analyst
nodes.summary: Applied behavior analysis, largely in autism and developmental care. Assessment, programming, and supervision.

nodes.label: Psychologist
nodes.summary: Doctoral mental health. Assessment, testing, therapy, and research. The clinical authority in behavioral health.

nodes.label:
Behavioral Health
Director
nodes.summary: Directs behavioral health services. Programs, compliance, crisis systems, and the workforce that runs them.

nodes.label:
Dental
Assistant
nodes.summary: Chairside support. Sterilization, radiographs, patient prep. The on-ramp to dental careers.

nodes.label:
Dental
Hygienist
nodes.summary: Preventive dental care. Cleanings, perio, patient education. Strong autonomy and pay for an associate degree.

nodes.label: Dentist
nodes.summary: The doctoral oral health clinician. Diagnoses, treats, and runs the practice. Often a small-business owner too.

nodes.label:
Sterile Processing
Technician
nodes.summary: Owns the instruments. Decontamination, sterilization, and the trays every surgery depends on. Invisible until it fails.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to surgical tech or lead

nodes.label:
Surgical
Technologist
nodes.summary: Scrubs in. Sterile field, instruments, anticipating the surgeon’s next move. The OR’s right hand.

nodes.label:
Surgical First
Assistant
nodes.summary: Hands in the field. Retraction, suturing, hemostasis. The step beyond scrubbing.

nodes.label:
Medical
Assistant
nodes.summary: The clinic’s utility player. Rooming, vitals, injections, and the front-to-back of an outpatient visit.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Bridge to nursing or other tracks

nodes.label:
Telemetry /
Monitor Tech
nodes.summary: Watches the rhythms. Reads telemetry, catches the arrhythmia, calls the nurse. Eyes that don’t blink.

nodes.label: Doula
nodes.summary: Continuous support through birth. Not clinical, deeply care. A growing, evidence-backed role in maternal outcomes.

nodes.label:
Lactation
Consultant
nodes.summary: The feeding expert. Clinical lactation support for new families. Often built on a nursing or allied base.

nodes.label:
EKG / Cardiographic
Technician
nodes.summary: Runs the 12-lead and stress tests. The first read on the heart. A short on-ramp to cardiovascular careers.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Advance to CV tech or echo

nodes.label:
Cardiovascular
Technologist
nodes.summary: Invasive and noninvasive cardiac diagnostics. Cath lab, vascular, and the data behind cardiology.

nodes.label: Echocardiographer
nodes.summary: Ultrasound of the heart. Structure, function, the images cardiology lives on.

nodes.label:
Electrophysiology
Technologist
nodes.summary: The rhythm lab. Ablations, device implants, mapping. High-skill cardiac procedural work.

nodes.label:
EEG / Neuro-
diagnostic Tech
nodes.summary: Records the brain. EEG, evoked potentials, intraoperative monitoring. The signal behind neurology.

nodes.label:
Polysomnographic
Technologist
nodes.summary: Runs the sleep study. Scores the stages, titrates the pressure. The diagnostic side of sleep medicine.

nodes.label:
Ophthalmic
Technician
nodes.summary: Eyes and imaging. Vision testing, OCT, fields, and the workup before the ophthalmologist.

nodes.label:
Pathologists’
Assistant
nodes.summary: Gross dissection and autopsy support under a pathologist. The bridge between the OR specimen and the diagnosis.

nodes.label:
Community Health
Worker
nodes.summary: The trusted neighbor inside the system. Outreach, education, and closing the gap between clinic and community.

nodes.label:
Medical Social
Worker
nodes.summary: The system’s problem-solver. Resources, discharge, crisis, and the social side of being sick. Master’s-level.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to case management or leadership

nodes.label:
Health
Coach
nodes.summary: Behavior change, one conversation at a time. Chronic disease, prevention, the daily habits that move outcomes.

nodes.label:
Peer Support
Specialist
nodes.summary: Lived experience as credential. Walks alongside people in recovery and serious illness. The role that proves you made it through.

nodes.label:
Chaplain /
Spiritual Care
nodes.summary: The presence at the hardest moments. Spiritual care, ethics, and family support across every faith and none.

nodes.label:
Unit Secretary
/ HUC
nodes.summary: Runs the desk that runs the unit. Orders, charts, phones, and the flow of a busy floor. A classic way in.

nodes.label:
Public Health
Aide / Worker
nodes.summary: Boots on the ground for a health department. Outreach, screenings, data collection, the work that keeps a population healthy before it ever sees a clinic.

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move into analysis or program work

nodes.label:
Public Health
Analyst
nodes.summary: Turns population data into action. Surveillance, grants, program evaluation. The analytical engine of a health department.

nodes.label: Epidemiologist
nodes.summary: The disease detective. Tracks outbreaks, models risk, and writes the evidence policy is supposed to follow. An MPH minimum, often doctoral.

req.title: Requirements
items: Master of Public Health or higher

mile.title: Key milestones
items: Move to state-level or director roles

nodes.label:
Public Health
Program Manager
nodes.summary: Runs a public health program. Budget, staff, grants, and the metrics a funder and a board both watch.

nodes.label:
Public Health
Director
nodes.summary: Runs a county or regional health department. Communicable disease, emergencies, the public’s health is the job description.

nodes.label:
Health
Commissioner
nodes.summary: The top public health official for a state or large jurisdiction. Often a physician. Where medicine, policy, and politics collide.

nodes.label:
Health Facility
Surveyor
nodes.summary: The state inspector. Walks hospitals and nursing homes against the regulations, writes the deficiencies, and holds the license. Usually a clinician who crossed over.

nodes.label:
Health Policy
Analyst
nodes.summary: Reads the bills, runs the numbers, writes the brief. Translates clinical reality into language a legislator can vote on. Where advocacy meets evidence.

nodes.label:
Health Policy
Advisor
nodes.summary: In the room where it happens. Advises legislators or executives on health policy, shapes legislation, and carries the clinician’s voice into the statute.

nodes.label:
Simulation
Educator
nodes.summary: Runs the sim lab. Mannequins, scenarios, and debriefs that let students fail safely before they ever touch a patient.

nodes.label:
Clinical
Instructor
nodes.summary: Takes students onto the floor. Bridges the classroom and the bedside, and signs off that a new clinician is safe to practice.

nodes.label:
Adjunct
Faculty
nodes.summary: A practitioner who teaches on the side. Brings the real job into the classroom, course by course. The most common way clinicians enter academia.

nodes.label:
Instructor /
Lecturer
nodes.summary: Full-time teaching faculty without the research mandate. Owns courses and students, the backbone of a teaching program.

nodes.label:
Assistant
Professor
nodes.summary: The tenure-track start. Teaching, research, and service, with the clock running toward tenure. A doctorate required.

nodes.label:
Associate
Professor
nodes.summary: Tenured. Established scholar, mentor, and program leader. The middle of a long academic career.

nodes.label: Professor
nodes.summary: The top of the academic ladder. National reputation, big grants, the scholars who train the trainers.

nodes.label:
Program
Director
nodes.summary: Runs an academic program, a nursing school, an RT program, an allied health track. Accreditation, curriculum, outcomes, and the students’ futures.

nodes.label:
Dean of
Health Sciences
nodes.summary: The academic executive. Runs a college of health sciences, faculty, budget, strategy, and the pipeline that feeds the whole workforce.

nodes.label:
Radiation
Therapist
nodes.summary: Delivers the prescribed dose, fraction by fraction, on a linear accelerator. Oncology's daily hands: the same patients for weeks, precision measured in millimeters.

nodes.label: Optician
nodes.summary: Fits and dispenses what the prescription calls for. Frames, lenses, adjustments, and the storefront of vision care.

req.title: Requirements
items: ABO exam, or state license where required

nodes.label:
RN Case
Manager
nodes.summary: Owns the discharge plan and the length of stay. Works the chart against InterQual, the plan against reality, and the payer against the clock.

req.title: Requirements
items: CCM eligibility: 12+ months full-time case management

nodes.label:
Massage
Therapist
nodes.summary: Licensed soft-tissue work, and not just the spa: clinical massage lives in rehab, oncology, and hospice settings. State boards run on the MBLEx.

req.title: Requirements
items: State-approved massage program (hours vary by state)

nodes.label:
Oncology
RN
nodes.summary: Chemo certification, ports and pumps, and patients you will know for months. Infusion, inpatient oncology, and radiation side by side.

req.title: Requirements
items: 1-2 years oncology practice for the OCN

diagnostic.label: Diagnostic
diagnostic.blurb: Reading the patient — imaging and labs.

patients.label: Patients
patients.blurb: The other side of the same system. Health is a path too. Three journeys here: general health from well to critical, the maternal journey through pregnancy and birth, and the newborn journey that begins where labor ends.

nodes.label:
Healthy /
Well
nodes.summary: No active disease. The patient meets the system through prevention, screening, and the occasional minor issue. The cheapest, healthiest, and most under-served state.

req.title: Defining features
items: Up to date on age-based screening

nodes.label:
At
Risk
nodes.summary: No diagnosis yet, but the trajectory is bending. Prediabetes, borderline pressures, lifestyle and genetic load. The highest-leverage place to intervene, and the easiest to ignore.

meta.v: Low if managed, high if not

mile.title: Triggers & transitions
items: Risk factors controlled, returns toward well

nodes.label:
Acute Illness
/ Injury
nodes.summary: Something happened. Infection, injury, an episode. Usually time-limited and resolvable, but the doorway to both recovery and the deep end.

nodes.label:
Chronic
Condition
nodes.summary: A diagnosis that doesn't leave. Diabetes, COPD, heart failure, hypertension. The state most of healthcare spending lives in, managed over years, not visits.

nodes.label:
Recovery
/ Rehab
nodes.summary: Healing back toward baseline. Rehab, follow-up, the work of returning. The branch that points back up the tree, when the system supports it.

nodes.label:
Complex /
Multi-Morbid
nodes.summary: Multiple chronic conditions stacking and interacting. The 5% of patients driving most of the cost. Care coordination becomes the whole game.

nodes.label:
Critical /
Decompensated
nodes.summary: Life-threatening, acute, all hands. The ICU, the code, the moment the system spends the most to hold a person here.

nodes.label:
Palliative /
End of Life
nodes.summary: Goals shift from cure to comfort and dignity. Symptom control, family support, and the part of medicine that asks what matters most.

mile.title: Triggers & transitions
items: Symptom control improves quality of life

nodes.label:
Preconception
& Planning
nodes.summary: Before pregnancy. Folic acid, chronic-condition control, the health a baby inherits before it exists. The cheapest place to change an outcome.

meta.v: Optimizing chronic conditions, no pregnancy yet

mile.title: Triggers & transitions
items: A chronic condition raises future risk

nodes.label:
Pregnant
(Prenatal)
nodes.summary: A monitored state, not an illness. Prenatal visits, screening, the long watch for the small fraction that goes sideways.

nodes.label:
High-Risk
Pregnancy
nodes.summary: Gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, multiples, a prior loss. Closer monitoring, specialist care, the pregnancies that need a maternal-fetal medicine team.

nodes.label:
Labor &
Delivery
nodes.summary: The event. Hours to days, the highest-acuity hours of an otherwise healthy life. Where the maternal journey hands off to the newborn one.

nodes.label:
Obstetric
Emergency
nodes.summary: Hemorrhage, eclampsia, embolism. The reason maternal mortality is a number people argue about. Minutes matter.

nodes.label: Postpartum
nodes.summary: The fourth trimester. Recovery, feeding, and the depression and hypertension that show up after everyone stopped watching.

nodes.label:
Healthy
Newborn
nodes.summary: First breath, first feed, first screen. The vast majority go home in two days. The journey everyone hopes stays boring.

nodes.label:
At-Risk
Newborn
nodes.summary: Late preterm, jaundice, poor feeding, a worried first week. Often a phototherapy light and a few extra days, sometimes the front door to the NICU.

nodes.label:
Sick
Infant
nodes.summary: Infection, dehydration, a bounce-back to the hospital. The acute illness of the first months, scary because the margins are so small.

nodes.label:
Congenital /
Chronic
nodes.summary: Congenital heart disease, prematurity sequelae, a feeding tube that goes home. The babies who become the complex kids, then complex adults.

req.title: Defining features
items: One or more congenital / chronic conditions

nodes.label:
NICU /
Critically Ill
nodes.summary: Prematurity, respiratory failure, the most fragile patients in the building. Weeks to months, and the respiratory therapists from the other track live here.

nodes.label:
Neonatal
Palliative
nodes.summary: When cure is not the goal. Comfort, dignity, and a family supported through the hardest thing there is.

infant.label: Newborn · Babies
infant.blurb: The first journey, from first breath.

skill.label: Skills
skill.blurb: Competencies you build — clinical, diagnostic, informatics, communication, leadership.

spec.label: Specializations
spec.blurb: Clinical focus areas — where you go deep.

experience.label: Experience
experience.blurb: Settings, populations, and the contexts you have worked.

nodes.label: Patient Assessment
nodes.summary: Head-to-toe and focused exams. Spotting the patient going bad before the monitor says so.

nodes.label: Vitals & Monitoring
nodes.summary: Trends over single numbers. Knowing what a creeping respiratory rate is telling you.

nodes.label: IV & Vascular Access
nodes.summary: Peripheral sticks, the hard ones, central line care. Makes you useful on any floor.

nodes.label: Medication Administration
nodes.summary: Five rights, high-alert drugs, titration. Where attention to detail is not optional.

nodes.label: Airway Management
nodes.summary: Positioning, suction, adjuncts, assisting intubation. The first letter of ABC.

nodes.label: Ventilator Management
nodes.summary: Modes, weaning, troubleshooting alarms. RT bread and butter, ICU survival skill.

nodes.label: CPR / ACLS
nodes.summary: Codes, rhythms, the algorithm under pressure. Stays calm when the room is not.
nodes.seen: Code teams, ED, ICU, cath lab

nodes.label: Wound Care
nodes.summary: Staging, dressings, offloading, when to escalate. Quiet skill, big outcomes.

nodes.label: EKG Interpretation
nodes.summary: Reading the strip, catching the STEMI, knowing artifact from arrhythmia.

nodes.label: Point-of-Care Testing
nodes.summary: Glucose, blood gas, rapid tests at the bedside. Fast answers, real consequences.

nodes.label: EHR Documentation
nodes.summary: Charting that holds up, order navigation, not drowning in clicks.

nodes.label: Clinical Informatics
nodes.summary: Build, workflow, data integrity. Making the system serve the clinician.

nodes.label: Data Analysis
nodes.summary: Pulling the report, reading the trend, telling the true story with numbers.

nodes.label: Medical Coding
nodes.summary: ICD-10, CPT, the language that turns care into a claim. Accuracy is the job.

nodes.label: Patient Education
nodes.summary: Teach-back, meeting people where they are. The part that changes outcomes.

nodes.label: Care Coordination
nodes.summary: Moving a patient through a fragmented system without dropping the handoff.

nodes.label: Handoff / SBAR
nodes.summary: The 30-second report that keeps the next shift from flying blind.

nodes.label: Quality Improvement
nodes.summary: PDSA, root cause, fixing the process instead of blaming the person.

nodes.label: Lean / Six Sigma
nodes.summary: Cutting waste, mapping the value stream, the ops side of better care.

nodes.label: Team Leadership
nodes.summary: Charge, precepting, holding a unit together on a short-staffed night.

nodes.label: Intensive Care
nodes.summary: The sickest patients, the tightest margins, one nurse to one or two.

nodes.label: Cardiac ICU
nodes.summary: Post-op hearts, drips, balloon pumps. Hemodynamics as a way of life.

nodes.label: Neuro ICU
nodes.summary: Strokes, bleeds, ICP. Where a neuro check every hour actually matters.

nodes.label: Neonatal ICU
nodes.summary: The smallest patients. Grams, not pounds. A whole different rulebook.

nodes.label: Pediatric ICU
nodes.summary: Critically ill kids. Weight-based everything, families in the room.

nodes.label: Emergency
nodes.summary: Undifferentiated, anything through the door, triage as a core skill.

nodes.label: Perioperative / OR
nodes.summary: Sterile field, the surgical count, a team that runs on choreography.

nodes.label: Cath Lab
nodes.summary: Door-to-balloon. The place a STEMI gets fixed.

nodes.label: Oncology
nodes.summary: Chemo, the long haul, presence as much as procedure.

nodes.label: Cardiology
nodes.summary: Rhythms, failure, the workhorse specialty of the hospital.

nodes.label: Nephrology / Dialysis
nodes.summary: Access, clearances, the three-times-a-week relationship with patients.

nodes.label: Labor & Delivery
nodes.summary: Two patients at once, fast turns from routine to emergency.

nodes.label: Pediatrics
nodes.summary: Kids are not small adults. Different doses, different fears, different wins.

nodes.label: Hospice / Palliative
nodes.summary: Comfort as the goal, hard conversations, dignity at the end.

nodes.label: Home Health
nodes.summary: Care in the living room, autonomy, the social determinants up close.

nodes.label: Behavioral Health
nodes.summary: De-escalation, safety, the most under-resourced corner of the system.

nodes.label: Rehabilitation
nodes.summary: Function over cure. The slow daily wins of getting someone back.

nodes.label: Academic Medical Center
nodes.summary: Teaching service, residents, the rare cases and the bureaucracy with them.

nodes.label: Community Hospital
nodes.summary: Bread-and-butter medicine, broad scope, you are the resource.

nodes.label: Critical Access / Rural
nodes.summary: Few beds, long transfers, doing a lot with a little. Generalist by necessity.

nodes.label: Long-Term Care
nodes.summary: Chronic, relational, the part of the system everyone forgets until they need it.

nodes.label: Ambulatory Clinic
nodes.summary: Outpatient pace, prevention, the front line of chronic disease.

nodes.label: Telehealth
nodes.summary: Care through a screen. New skills, new access, new failure modes.

nodes.label: Geriatrics
nodes.summary: Polypharmacy, falls, goals of care. The population that is only growing.

nodes.label: Neonatal
nodes.summary: Newborns, the first hours, the highest stakes per ounce.

nodes.label: Adult Med-Surg
nodes.summary: The training ground. Five-plus patients, every system, every shift different.

nodes.label: Bariatric
nodes.summary: Equipment, dignity, the care gaps a bias-laden system leaves behind.

nodes.label: Veterans / VA
nodes.summary: Service-connected conditions, a system of its own, a population owed a lot.

nodes.label: Trauma Center
nodes.summary: Levels, the golden hour, organized chaos with a protocol underneath.

nodes.label: Disaster / Mass Casualty
nodes.summary: Triage at scale, when the surge breaks the normal rules.

nodes.label: Transport / Flight
nodes.summary: Critical care at altitude, alone, with what you brought.
nodes.seen: Flight and ground critical care transport

nodes.label: Corrections
nodes.summary: Care behind the wall. Constraint, mistrust, and a real duty anyway.

nodes.label: Travel Nursing
nodes.summary: Drop into a new unit, find the supply room, be competent by lunch.

nodes.label: Urinary Catheterization
nodes.summary: Foley insertion and care, sterile field, the right size. Watching for the infection you can cause.

nodes.label: NG / Feeding Tubes
nodes.summary: Placement, confirmation, and feeds. Aspiration is the thing you are always watching for.

nodes.label: Phlebotomy
nodes.summary: Drawing blood without blowing the vein. Everyone underrates it until they need it.

nodes.label: Sterile Technique
nodes.summary: Setting and holding a sterile field. The difference between a clean procedure and an infection.

nodes.label: Isolation & PPE
nodes.summary: Donning, doffing, and not contaminating yourself. Got very real in 2020.

nodes.label: Telemetry Monitoring
nodes.summary: Reading the rhythm strip and knowing when it actually matters.

nodes.label: Chest Tube Management
nodes.summary: Setting up the drainage, watching the water seal, knowing when to worry.

nodes.label: Central Line Care
nodes.summary: Dressing changes, access, and keeping CLABSI off your unit.

nodes.label: Tracheostomy Care
nodes.summary: Suctioning, changing ties, managing a tube someone is breathing through.

nodes.label: Blood Transfusion
nodes.summary: Checking the unit, watching for a reaction, documenting every step.

nodes.label: Restraints & Patient Safety
nodes.summary: Least restrictive, frequent checks, real documentation. Safety without losing dignity.

nodes.label: Fall Prevention
nodes.summary: Rounding, the bed alarm, knowing which patient is going to try to stand.

nodes.label: ABG Interpretation
nodes.summary: Reading the blood gas. Acidosis, alkalosis, and what the lungs and kidneys are doing about it.

nodes.label: Point-of-Care Ultrasound
nodes.summary: Ultrasound at the bedside. Fast looks and line placement that used to require radiology.

nodes.label: Pulmonary Function Testing
nodes.summary: Spirometry, lung volumes, the numbers behind a pulmonary diagnosis.

nodes.label: Lab Interpretation
nodes.summary: Reading the panel. Which value is the emergency and which is the recheck.

nodes.label: Imaging Basics
nodes.summary: Knowing what a film shows and what to escalate. Not reading like a radiologist, reading like a clinician.

nodes.label: Epic / Chronicles Build
nodes.summary: Building the workflow inside Epic. Where clinical reality meets the database.

nodes.label: Cerner / Oracle Health
nodes.summary: The other major EHR. Same idea, different build language.

nodes.label: SQL & Querying
nodes.summary: Pulling the data yourself instead of waiting on a report.

nodes.label: Dashboards & BI
nodes.summary: Tableau, Power BI, turning a data pull into something a leader will actually read.

nodes.label: Clinical Decision Support
nodes.summary: The alerts and order sets that nudge care. Build them well or everyone clicks past them.

nodes.label: Interoperability (HL7/FHIR)
nodes.summary: Getting systems to talk. The plumbing nobody sees until it breaks.

nodes.label: Privacy & Security
nodes.summary: Access, audit, and keeping the record safe. Boring until there is a breach.

nodes.label: De-escalation
nodes.summary: Talking someone down before it becomes a code gray. Tone, space, and not taking the bait.

nodes.label: Motivational Interviewing
nodes.summary: Meeting the patient where they are. Change talk instead of lecturing.

nodes.label: Goals-of-Care Talks
nodes.summary: The hard conversation about what comes next. Honest, unhurried, on the patient’s terms.

nodes.label: Interpreter & Health Literacy
nodes.summary: Using an interpreter right and writing at a level people actually understand.

nodes.label: Presenting & Teaching
nodes.summary: Standing up in front of the room and making it land. Staff ed, rounds, the board meeting.

nodes.label: Precepting & Mentoring
nodes.summary: Bringing up the next person. The unit runs on who you trained.

nodes.label: Staffing & Scheduling
nodes.summary: Matching bodies to acuity. Everyone’s least favorite math and the one that matters most.

nodes.label: Budgeting & Finance
nodes.summary: Cost centers, productivity, defending your FTEs. The language leadership listens in.

nodes.label: Project Management
nodes.summary: Scope, timeline, the rollout that actually lands. PMP if you go formal.

nodes.label: Change Management
nodes.summary: Getting people to adopt the new thing. The tech is easy, the humans are not.

nodes.label: Policy & Compliance
nodes.summary: Writing the standard and keeping the unit on the right side of the regs.

nodes.label: Evidence-Based Practice
nodes.summary: Putting the literature into the protocol instead of doing it the way it has always been done.

nodes.label: Research / IRB
nodes.summary: Running a study, protecting subjects, surviving the IRB.

nodes.label: Biostatistics
nodes.summary: Reading the study and knowing whether the result means anything.

nodes.label: Burn ICU
nodes.summary: Burn injury, fluid resuscitation, and wound care at the deepest end.

nodes.label: Transplant
nodes.summary: Pre and post organ transplant. Immunosuppression, rejection, the long game.

nodes.label: ECMO
nodes.summary: The lung-and-heart bypass of last resort. High stakes, high training.

nodes.label: Surgical ICU
nodes.summary: Post-op critical care, the fresh belly, the unstable surgical patient.

nodes.label: PACU / Recovery
nodes.summary: Waking patients up safely. Airway, pain, the first hour after anesthesia.

nodes.label: Endoscopy / GI
nodes.summary: Scopes, sedation, and turning the room over fast.

nodes.label: Interventional Radiology
nodes.summary: Image-guided procedures. The catheter that fixes it without the OR.

nodes.label: Electrophysiology
nodes.summary: Ablations, devices, and the electrical wiring of the heart.

nodes.label: Wound / Ostomy
nodes.summary: The wound that will not heal and the ostomy the patient has to live with.

nodes.label: Infusion / IV Therapy
nodes.summary: Chemo, biologics, long-term access. The chair, the pump, the protocol.

nodes.label: Apheresis
nodes.summary: Pulling blood, separating it, giving it back. Donation and treatment both.

nodes.label: Pulmonology
nodes.summary: Lungs, ventilators, the chronic breather. RT’s home turf.

nodes.label: Gastroenterology
nodes.summary: Guts, scopes, livers. The specialty that sees everything you eat.

nodes.label: Endocrinology / Diabetes
nodes.summary: Hormones, insulin, the slow chronic management that keeps people out of the hospital.

nodes.label: Infectious Disease
nodes.summary: Bugs and drugs. Stewardship, isolation, the outbreak nobody wants.

nodes.label: Nephrology
nodes.summary: Kidneys, fluids, electrolytes. Where dialysis decisions get made.

nodes.label: Pain Management
nodes.summary: The hard balance of relief and risk. Procedures, meds, and a lot of judgment.

nodes.label: Sleep Medicine
nodes.summary: Apnea, studies, the CPAP nobody wants to wear.

nodes.label: Geriatric Medicine
nodes.summary: Polypharmacy, frailty, and what actually matters at the end of a long life.

nodes.label: Postpartum / Mother-Baby
nodes.summary: The first days. Recovery, feeding, and teaching two patients at once.

nodes.label: Antepartum / High-Risk OB
nodes.summary: The pregnancy that needs watching. Bed rest, monitoring, the long admission.

nodes.label: Lactation
nodes.summary: Feeding support that decides whether breastfeeding works. IBCLC if you go formal.

nodes.label: Pediatric Specialty
nodes.summary: Kids are not small adults. Doses, development, and the parent in the room.

nodes.label: Public Health
nodes.summary: Population over patient. Outbreaks, prevention, the health of a whole community.

nodes.label: School Health
nodes.summary: One nurse, a building full of kids, and everything from asthma to a broken arm.

nodes.label: Occupational Health
nodes.summary: Keeping workers healthy and on the job. Injuries, screenings, return-to-work.

nodes.label: Forensic / SANE
nodes.summary: Evidence, exams, and care for the patient who is also a case.

nodes.label: Addiction / Recovery
nodes.summary: Substance use treatment. MAT, withdrawal, and meeting people without judgment.

nodes.label: Telehealth
nodes.summary: Care through a screen. Triage, follow-up, the visit that did not need a drive.

nodes.label: Skilled Nursing Facility
nodes.summary: Post-acute care, rehab, and the long stay. Where hospital discharge actually lands.

nodes.label: Long-Term Acute Care
nodes.summary: The patient too sick for a SNF, too stable for the ICU. Vents and complex wounds.

nodes.label: Inpatient Rehab
nodes.summary: Three hours of therapy a day and the road back to function.

nodes.label: Ambulatory Surgery Center
nodes.summary: Same-day surgery, in and out. Lean, fast, profitable.

nodes.label: Urgent Care
nodes.summary: The middle ground between clinic and ED. Sutures, strep, and a lot of x-rays.

nodes.label: Community Health Center
nodes.summary: Care for everyone regardless of pay. The safety net, doing a lot with a little.

nodes.label: Dialysis Center
nodes.summary: The same patients, three days a week, for years. A relationship and a routine.

nodes.label: Physician Office
nodes.summary: Primary care, the panel, the long-term relationship with a patient and a family.

nodes.label: Retail / Pharmacy Clinic
nodes.summary: The clinic in the drugstore. Vaccines, minor illness, convenience care.

nodes.label: Pediatrics
nodes.summary: Newborn to teenager. A different patient and a different parent at every age.

nodes.label: Women’s Health
nodes.summary: Reproductive, prenatal, and the care a system too often shortchanges.

nodes.label: Oncology Patients
nodes.summary: Cancer, start to finish. Cure, chronic, or comfort, and walking it with them.

nodes.label: Behavioral Health Patients
nodes.summary: Mental illness and crisis. The population the rest of the system keeps failing.

nodes.label: Homeless / Underserved
nodes.summary: Care without a safety net under it. Hard, necessary, and clarifying.

nodes.label: Substance Use
nodes.summary: The patient everyone has an opinion about. Withdrawal, MAT, and meeting them straight.

nodes.label: End-of-Life
nodes.summary: The last chapter, done well. Comfort, family, and presence over fixing.

nodes.label: Float Pool
nodes.summary: A different unit every shift. You learn the whole hospital or you sink.

nodes.label: Charge Nurse
nodes.summary: Running the floor. Assignments, fires, and being the person everyone asks.

nodes.label: Rapid Response / Code Team
nodes.summary: The page nobody wants. Walking into a room going bad and steadying it.

nodes.label: Night Shift
nodes.summary: Fewer people, more autonomy, and a body clock you fight forever.

nodes.label: Magnet Hospital
nodes.summary: The designation for nursing excellence. Shared governance and a higher bar.

nodes.label: Digital Health / Startup
nodes.summary: Healthcare meets the startup. Move fast, build the thing, and learn the regs the hard way.

nodes.label: Military / VA
nodes.summary: Serving those who served. Trauma, systems, and a mission on top of the medicine.

nodes.label: Global / Humanitarian
nodes.summary: Care where the resources are not. Improvise, prioritize, and do the most good.

nodes.label: Step-Down / Progressive
nodes.summary: Sicker than the floor, not quite the ICU. The in-between that needs sharp eyes.

nodes.label: Research Site
nodes.summary: Protocols, consent, and care that doubles as data.

nodes.label: Healthy / Well
nodes.summary: No active disease. The patient meets the system through prevention, screening, and the occasional minor issue. The cheapest, healthiest, and most under-served state.

nodes.label: At Risk
nodes.summary: No diagnosis yet, but the trajectory is bending. Prediabetes, borderline pressures, lifestyle and genetic load. The highest-leverage place to intervene, and the easiest to ignore.

nodes.label: Acute Illness / Injury
nodes.summary: Something happened. Infection, injury, an episode. Usually time-limited and resolvable, but the doorway to both recovery and the deep end.

nodes.label: Chronic Condition
nodes.summary: A diagnosis that doesn't leave. Diabetes, COPD, heart failure, hypertension. The state most of healthcare spending lives in, managed over years, not visits.

nodes.label: Recovery / Rehab
nodes.summary: Healing back toward baseline. Rehab, follow-up, the work of returning. The branch that points back up the tree, when the system supports it.

nodes.label: Complex / Multi-Morbid
nodes.summary: Multiple chronic conditions stacking and interacting. The 5% of patients driving most of the cost. Care coordination becomes the whole game.

nodes.label: Critical / Decompensated
nodes.summary: Life-threatening, acute, all hands. The ICU, the code, the moment the system spends the most to hold a person here.

nodes.label: Palliative / End of Life
nodes.summary: Goals shift from cure to comfort and dignity. Symptom control, family support, and the part of medicine that asks what matters most.

nodes.label: Preconception & Planning
nodes.summary: Before pregnancy. Folic acid, chronic-condition control, the health a baby inherits before it exists. The cheapest place to change an outcome.

nodes.label: Pregnant (Prenatal)
nodes.summary: A monitored state, not an illness. Prenatal visits, screening, the long watch for the small fraction that goes sideways.

nodes.label: High-Risk Pregnancy
nodes.summary: Gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, multiples, a prior loss. Closer monitoring, specialist care, the pregnancies that need a maternal-fetal medicine team.

nodes.label: Labor & Delivery
nodes.summary: The event. Hours to days, the highest-acuity hours of an otherwise healthy life. Where the maternal journey hands off to the newborn one.

nodes.label: Obstetric Emergency
nodes.summary: Hemorrhage, eclampsia, embolism. The reason maternal mortality is a number people argue about. Minutes matter.

nodes.label: Postpartum
nodes.summary: The fourth trimester. Recovery, feeding, and the depression and hypertension that show up after everyone stopped watching.

nodes.label: Healthy Newborn
nodes.summary: First breath, first feed, first screen. The vast majority go home in two days. The journey everyone hopes stays boring.

nodes.label: At-Risk Newborn
nodes.summary: Late preterm, jaundice, poor feeding, a worried first week. Often a phototherapy light and a few extra days, sometimes the front door to the NICU.

nodes.label: Sick Infant
nodes.summary: Infection, dehydration, a bounce-back to the hospital. The acute illness of the first months, scary because the margins are so small.

nodes.label: Congenital / Chronic
nodes.summary: Congenital heart disease, prematurity sequelae, a feeding tube that goes home. The babies who become the complex kids, then complex adults.

nodes.label: NICU / Critically Ill
nodes.summary: Prematurity, respiratory failure, the most fragile patients in the building. Weeks to months, and the respiratory therapists from the other track live here.

nodes.label: Neonatal Palliative
nodes.summary: When cure is not the goal. Comfort, dignity, and a family supported through the hardest thing there is.

nodes.label: Inpatient Psychiatric Facility
nodes.summary: Locked units, milieu management, safety checks every 15 minutes, and de-escalation as a core skill.

nodes.label: Children's Hospital
nodes.summary: Everything sized for kids, families in every room, and weight-based dosing on every single order.

nodes.label: Freestanding ED
nodes.summary: A full emergency department with no hospital attached, so anyone sick enough gets packaged and transferred.

nodes.label: Value-Based Care / ACO
nodes.summary: Paid for outcomes instead of volume, which changes what the organization asks you to chase every day.

nodes.label: PACE Program
nodes.summary: All-inclusive care that keeps nursing-home-eligible elders living at home, one interdisciplinary team per participant.

nodes.label: Hospital at Home
nodes.summary: Inpatient-level care delivered in the living room, with remote monitoring and daily in-person visits.
nodes.seen: CMS Acute Hospital Care at Home waiver sites

nodes.label: Patient-Centered Medical Home
nodes.summary: Primary care organized around team-based panels, with care gaps worked down huddle by huddle.

nodes.label: Mobile Integrated Health
nodes.summary: Community paramedicine, treating people where they live so they stop cycling through the ED.

nodes.label: Payer / Utilization Review
nodes.summary: The insurance side of the chart, running admissions against InterQual or MCG criteria all day.

nodes.label: PRN / Per Diem / Locum
nodes.summary: You pick the shifts, skip the meetings, and walk into a different unit cold every week.

nodes.label: Union Environment
nodes.summary: Contracts govern staffing ratios, floating, and grievances, and you learn what a steward does fast.

nodes.label: Joint Commission Survey
nodes.summary: Surveyors tracing real patients through the building while everyone rechecks crash carts and open vial dating.

nodes.label: CMS / State Survey
nodes.summary: The unannounced one with Conditions of Participation on the line, often triggered by a complaint.

nodes.label: Survey Readiness / Tracers
nodes.summary: Running mock tracers and closing findings before the real surveyors find them for you.

nodes.label: New Grad Residency
nodes.summary: A structured first year with preceptors, cohort classes, and someone actually checking on you.

nodes.label: Certified Stroke Center
nodes.summary: Door-to-needle clocks, stroke alerts, and NIHSS documentation the certifying body will audit.
nodes.seen: TJC and DNV certified stroke programs

nodes.label: Dementia / Memory Care
nodes.summary: Behavior is communication here, and half the care plan is really about the family.

nodes.label: Refugee / Immigrant Health
nodes.summary: Interpreters, trauma histories, and a health record that starts the day they arrived.

nodes.label: LGBTQ+ Health
nodes.summary: Getting names, hormones, and screening right for people the system has historically gotten wrong.

nodes.label: Limited English Proficiency
nodes.summary: Nothing happens without a qualified interpreter, and family members do not count as one.

nodes.label: Rural / Frontier Patients
nodes.summary: Ninety miles to the nearest specialist, so the plan has to survive the drive home.

nodes.label: Intellectual / Dev Disabilities
nodes.summary: Adjusting assessment, consent, and communication for patients whose baseline is not in the chart.

nodes.label: Diabetes Journey
nodes.summary: From prediabetes counseling to insulin titration to the foot ulcer that did not have to happen.

nodes.label: Heart Failure Journey
nodes.summary: Daily weights, GDMT titration, and the 30-day readmission window everyone upstairs is watching.

nodes.label: COPD Journey
nodes.summary: Exacerbation, home oxygen qualification, pulmonary rehab, repeat, hopefully with fewer admissions each cycle.

nodes.label: CKD / ESRD Journey
nodes.summary: Staging, access planning before the fistula is urgent, then dialysis or the transplant list.

nodes.label: Cancer Survivorship
nodes.summary: Treatment ended but surveillance, late effects, and the fear of recurrence did not.

nodes.label: Transplant Journey
nodes.summary: Evaluation, the waitlist, the call, then immunosuppression and lab draws for the rest of their life.

nodes.label: SDOH Screening & Referral
nodes.summary: Screening for housing, food, and transportation because the discharge plan fails without them.
nodes.seen: Admission assessments, Z codes, CMS measures

nodes.label: Patient Positioning
nodes.summary: Getting the anatomy on the plate right the first time. Repeats are dose.

nodes.label: Radiation Safety / ALARA
nodes.summary: Time, distance, shielding. The dose you save is usually your own.

nodes.label: Contrast Administration
nodes.summary: Screening kidneys and allergies, running the injector, watching for the reaction nobody expects.

nodes.label: Image Quality Control
nodes.summary: Exposure indices, artifacts, repeat analysis. Knowing why an image is bad, not just that it is.

nodes.label: MRI Safety Screening
nodes.summary: Implants, fragments, oxygen tanks. Zone IV forgives nothing ferromagnetic.

nodes.label: Specimen Processing
nodes.summary: Accessioning, spinning, aliquoting, chasing mislabels. The result is only as good as the tube.

nodes.label: Microscopy & Differentials
nodes.summary: Manual diffs, urine sediment, the smear the analyzer flagged and could not call.

nodes.label: Blood Bank / Crossmatch
nodes.summary: Type and screen, antibody workups, releasing units at 3 AM. Zero tolerance for shortcuts.

nodes.label: Micro Culture Reading
nodes.summary: Gram stains, plate reading, susceptibilities. Slow answers that change antibiotics.

nodes.label: Lab QC / Levey-Jennings
nodes.summary: Westgard rules, shifts and trends, holding results when the control is out.

nodes.label: LIS Operation
nodes.summary: Result entry, autoverification rules, interface errors. The lab's version of the EHR.

nodes.label: Sterile Compounding (USP 797)
nodes.summary: Hoods, garbing, beyond-use dating. The cleanroom does not care that you are behind.

nodes.label: Medication Reconciliation
nodes.summary: What they actually take versus what the list says. Admission and discharge are where errors live.

nodes.label: IV Admixture
nodes.summary: Compatibility, dilution, drips built right. Aseptic math under time pressure.

nodes.label: Controlled Substance Handling
nodes.summary: Counts, waste witnessing, diversion monitoring. The paper trail is the point.

nodes.label: Pharmacokinetic Dosing
nodes.summary: Vanc and aminoglycoside levels, renal adjustments, dosing to a curve instead of a habit.

nodes.label: Dispensing & Order Entry
nodes.summary: Filling, verification queues, insurance rejects. High volume, no margin for the wrong strength.

nodes.label: Gait Training
nodes.summary: Getting people walking again with a belt, a walker, and a plan for the bad step.

nodes.label: Transfer Training
nodes.summary: Bed to chair without hurting the patient or your own back. Mechanics before muscle.

nodes.label: ADL Retraining
nodes.summary: Dressing, bathing, feeding. Teaching independence back one task at a time.

nodes.label: Therapeutic Modalities
nodes.summary: E-stim, ultrasound, heat and ice. Adjuncts that earn their place or get dropped.

nodes.label: Home Assessment & DME
nodes.summary: Walkers, ramps, grab bars, what the house actually allows. Discharge planning meets the front porch.
nodes.seen: OT, PT, home health, case mgmt

nodes.label: Dysphagia Screening
nodes.summary: Bedside swallow evals, diet textures, aspiration risk. Quiet skill that prevents pneumonias.

nodes.label: Surgical Instrumentation
nodes.summary: Knowing the tray, passing before it is asked for, anticipating the case.

nodes.label: Counts & Time-Out
nodes.summary: Sponges, sharps, the universal protocol. Boring exactly until it is not.

nodes.label: Prepping & Draping
nodes.summary: Skin prep, sterile field setup, guarding the margins all case long.

nodes.label: First Assist Skills
nodes.summary: Retraction, hemostasis, closing. The second set of hands the surgeon actually trusts.
nodes.seen: RNFA, CSFA, PAs in the OR

nodes.label: Sterile Processing Workflow
nodes.summary: Decontam, set assembly, biological indicators. Every case upstairs starts down here.

nodes.label: Extrication & Scene Safety
nodes.summary: Scene size-up, packaging, getting the patient out without making a second one.

nodes.label: Field Triage (START)
nodes.summary: Sorting many patients with little time and less information. Walk, breathe, perfuse, follow commands.

nodes.label: Field 12-Lead Acquisition
nodes.summary: A clean tracing in a moving truck, transmitted before arrival. Minutes off the door-to-balloon clock.

nodes.label: Nutrition Assessment
nodes.summary: Malnutrition criteria, focused physical exam, intake that matches the chart or does not.

nodes.label: Enteral & Parenteral Nutrition
nodes.summary: Tube feed regimens, TPN monitoring, refeeding risk. Feeding people who cannot eat.

nodes.label: Sonography
nodes.summary: Real-time imaging that depends entirely on the hands holding the probe.

nodes.label: CT
nodes.summary: The fast cross-sectional workhorse. Stroke and trauma live on the scanner clock.

nodes.label: MRI
nodes.summary: Long scans, exquisite detail, a magnet that is always on.

nodes.label: Nuclear Medicine
nodes.summary: Injected isotopes, physiology over anatomy. PET and SPECT.

nodes.label: Radiation Therapy
nodes.summary: Fractionated treatment, millimeter setups, the same patient every weekday for weeks.

nodes.label: Crisis / 988
nodes.summary: Suicide risk, de-escalation by phone or in person, warm handoffs that actually connect.

nodes.label: Eating Disorders
nodes.summary: Medical stabilization plus behavioral work, refeeding done carefully, families in the room.
